kicker: ET LEVEL II // FLAW SIZING & FREQUENCY

beats.narration: Detection found the indication. Sizing decides what happens to the part — and eddy current hands you two independent rulers to measure it.

payload.title: TWO RULERS FOR A FLAW
payload.sub: Phase reads where it lives. Volts read how much moved.

beats.narration: Both are comparisons, never measurements. Each rides a calibration curve built from artificial flaws of known depth in a reference standard matched to your product.

payload.heading: Pick the ruler by the question

beats.narration: Phase lag is a clock. Deeper currents arrive late, near fifty-seven degrees per standard depth, doubled on the round trip. Rotate the through-wall hole to forty.

payload.heading: Angle becomes depth on the curve
payload.note: OD BRANCH SPREADS ~120° · ID CROWDS INTO 0–40°

beats.narration: Read the curve like a Level Two. OD signals spread over a hundred-plus degrees, so phase sizes them well. ID crowds tight — supplement it.

payload.heading: Where each ruler earns its keep

chips.s: Match volts to a wear scar

beats.narration: On continuous product, flaws arrive on a schedule. Frequency equals speed over spacing — and spacing over pi names the guilty roll.

payload.sub: Standardize through the coil at production speed — or the band misses it.
